title: Campaigns - Matt Western
meta_description: Member of Parliament for Warwick, Leamington, Whitnash and surrounding villages.
headings: h2: Let Matt Know What Matters to You
h2: Radford Semele Solar Farm Petition
text_excerpt: Campaigns - Matt Western Call on: 01926 882006 About News Western in Westminster Out and about Campaigns Meet Matt Get involved Cost-Of-Living Campaigns Let Matt Know What Matters to You Matt works to make positive change for the Warwick and Leamington community, so that’s why he wants to know what matters to you. Let Matt know what matters to you here to help him focus on matters that you want resolving. Radford Semele Solar Farm Petition 482 Signatures On Tuesday, 15th October, developers submitted a planning application for a 212-acre solar farm to be located southeast of Radford Semele, Leamington Spa. The proposal includes the installation of 75,300 solar panels, with a generating capacity of 49.99 megawatts (MW), and a request for a 40-year lease on the site. For further details about the application, you can click here. After reviewing the plans, meeting with the developers, listening to the concerns of local residents, and consulting with key stakeholders, I believe that Warwick District Council’s planning committee should not approve this application. While I acknowledge the importance of this project in contributing to the UK Government’s renewable energy goals and addressing the climate crisis, I am concerned that the developers have been misleading and disingenuous in their approach. By signing this petition, you will join me in urging the review and refusal of this proposal to develop a 212-acre solar farm southeast of Radford Semele, Leamington Spa, due to the following concerns: its size , the minor deviance of 0.01MW below the acceptable tolerance stipulated by the Government’s 50MW threshold should not diminish the project’s qualification as a Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project (NSIP); its scale , the immediacy of 75,300 solar panels to a substantial village;? the cumulative impact of several large-scale solar farms in the local area , government data suggests that 3 additional solar farms, are either operational or being developed within 2 miles of the site; the use of predominantly ‘best’ and ‘most versatile’ agricultural land , which should be protected from such industrial developments; its impact on the recreational amenity of the residents , there are 2 footpaths leading out into the countryside that are valued by local residents; the lack of proper community inclusion in the proposal, planning and consumption of this local energy scheme. In its current form, the proposed solar farm offers little to no benefit to residents who live near the site, such as cheaper energy bills. Please sign the petition below: Sign Petition STOP East Whitnash – put communities first, not developers UPDATE: On Tuesday 16th April, the East Whitnash development was granted planning permission. This is a deeply disappointing update but does not mean we will stop our campaign. We have fought this for 9 years and will continue. You can… Read On PROTECT our waterways! A petition calling for the dumping of sewage in our waterways to be stopped following over 6,000 hours of sewage being dumped into the River Avon and River Leam in 2022. We the undersigned: Demand the Government takes proper action… Read On SAVE our railway ticket offices Please speak up! Ministers should stop dodging accountability, halt this sham consultation, and come clean on the damage these closures will do. Over 150 million passenger journeys will be hit by this move, and yet there have been no equality… Read On Warwickshire, Let’s not be Run by Brum A petition to stop the absorption of Warwickshire County Council (WCC) into the West Midlands Combined Authority (WMCA). We the undersigned: Demand that Warwickshire is not absorbed into the WMCA and run by a Mayor almost 30 miles away. Insist… Read On error: © Matt Western MP 2018 Website by image+ Home Contact Media enquiries Privacy Terms Call: 01926 882006
